title: Elymus thoroldianus
intro: Elymus thoroldianus, also known as Thorold's wheatgrass, is a perennial grass species native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as potential applications in wound healing and cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Elymus thoroldianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elymus thoroldianus is commonly prepared as a tincture or a tea, and is also used in topical applications for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Elymus thoroldianus is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Elymus thoroldianus is further divided into the genus Elymus, which belongs to the tribe Triticeae, and the kingdom Plantae. Elymus thoroldianus is also known as the Barley grass. It is a type of perennial grass.
